title: Talking About 24 hours of Moab Solo Mountain Bike Race 2010 - 1stx 1/4

transcript.text: Jesus okay
Hello these are this is my experience with my first 24-hour mountain bike race
Um the 24 hours of moab figured I'd do a video while I'm still fresh
Ooh in my mind um it's been almost a week or did it last weekend and uh saturday and sunday and
It's thursday now so um yeah
Um it's fun um as far um as the training goes
I um it was a 2010 uh 24 hours of moab and I got 16th out of men as far as training goes um
It was bike touring um I was in I was in shape before
I lifted weights and I gained weight from 160 to 190 pounds because I was gonna do this kayak trip
And my friend flaked on me and and um I got like two and um addicted to some computer project
So I didn't really do any exercise for two months and then I did a marathon but I only made it 13 miles
Um and then um
I uh came home in march I rode like maybe a dozen times or something and then I rode europe
Um I biked two or three europe from like uh amsterdam down to uh athens and back up to the
Northern tip of norway and back down to amsterdam I took the train for some of it though like from
Bulgaria to helsinki I took the train and the bus because I was taking too much time in cities and
Stuff but anyways um so that was the training that I did for it to get in shape I managed to drop I
Don't know how many miles that was maybe 10 000 or something my computers kept broke breaking so
But basically that was my training um so I was in I was in shape um but the last time I really rode was
Like the 10th of september and the race was like the 9th of september so I had like a month of not
Riding with the exception of five days of cruising around samsterdam and then I went on like three
Mountain bike rides like two weeks before the race checking out the course and stuff um getting
Figuring out my lines and stuff but then my frame cracked and so I couldn't ride anymore I had a
Backup bike but I had no suspension I wasn't really motivated so I just went into town and just
Spent the week and a half like painting my road bike rainbow colors because it was rusted um
And uh so then my family came down and did the tour the crew for me and I came down on friday
And by the time I came down I was completely fresh um the three days of mountain biking that I did
On the course killed me um it actually made me sick for three days where I couldn't even do anything for
Three days because I was on a 26 inch you know hard tail you know 13 year old bike and it which is
I found difficult on that course because it uh kept because it would get stuck in the sand and then I
I would get stuck in the rocks and uh I went mountain biking with some guy who went down there michael
Cook from austin who was doing on a team and he was he had a 29 inch full suspension bike and he's like
Dude you gotta get one of these these are way better they roll so much better and I could tell
That I was beating him on the uh technical but then he would like zoom by me on the uh on the flats and
Stuff and through the sand pits so I was like all right so I went into town and I and I reserved a
Bike to work to to race it a full suspension 29 inch um santa cruz tall boy medium size
And uh I'm so glad that I did that that made it a million times easier um and uh so
Those guys said something about they were the guys at the bike shop were like giving me pointers on like
What to bring like as far as how many cartridges and um tubes and stuff and they're like yeah so if you do
10 laps that's 150 miles so you know that's a lot of like uh opportunity to get a flat and stuff so
When my family were like how many laps you can do I was like oh I guess 100 I guess 10 because that's
What the good people the bike shop said but in my mind I was gonna shoot for 13 or 14 um
Just because that's what like the strongest girl does and I figured I I could do about like that um
And um so I uh my mom came and she got a hotel for us
Um and she gave me she did some light treatment on me on this like really expensive like light thing
Where she put over my stomach and my legs and it made me groggy I had been having like really bad
Insomnia for a few days up to that I was kind of worried but that made me kind of groggy and then
She gave me four benadryls so that made me groggy and I managed to sleep that night
Which was a major bonus if you ever do a 24-hour solo you've got to sleep like a baby the night before
Even if it means taking a half dozen sleeping pills because even if you're totally groggy
When the race starts because of the sleeping pills all that matters is you slept um because even if
You're totally groggy you're still the adrenaline's going to override that grogginess because um we got
Up at like seven and then I had like what I had for breakfast I had they had three waffles at the
A passionately hell that we stayed it and um I had like a couple cups of coffee I had like three waffles
A thing of oat of um yogurt two apples um
And um and then as as the race was getting closer maybe out of nervousness or just out of trying to be
Extra safe I downed like three um in gels and one even as close as like 20 minutes before it and I
Was drinking that I was popping those emergency electrolytes I had like two of them or something
And so we all lined up for the race and um the adrenaline started pumping in my blood like no matter
How uh environment my the adrenaline just started uh pumping and um so um then uh
Yeah the thing the gun went off and it's this big lehman style start where you run like 150 meters up
Around this tree and then back to the bike and I was running as hard as I fast as I possibly could and I
Was still like 100th person to get to the bike I mean people running so fast but one reason that I
I wasn't running as fast as I could and everybody's running past me was because I hadn't um I hadn't
Gotten any exercise for like two weeks before that so like I was kind of out of uh
It was rusty um because I got on my bike like the night before I rented the bike
And I rode my bike as hard as I could down the uh down the block just to see if I was strong and
I could tell that my leg speed had gone and gone bye-bye and also my uh I felt like uh that feeling of uh
Where you get the burn all over your body and then you get like out of breath really quickly like
You're out of shape so that kind of I kind of it had been long enough for me to
Uh so maybe that's why I just wasn't able to run that fast but it didn't even matter anyways
Because uh you're supposed to you know pace yourself but then I got a bike and I rode
I could see like the guys in the front of the pack like up there like a quarter mile ahead of me you
Know like in a pack like uh going down the dirt road like it's a road race or something
And I was kind of like sort of I guess you could call it a bunch of straggler guys like not really
Forming a group or anything but I was kind of drafting off some dude a little bit you know
But I could barely kind of hang on to my leg speed was gone and then I was going um up the hill
And then I went down this downhill and at the exact same place where I crashed 11 years ago because I did
This race as part of a team they invited me at the last minute in 99 and um they had they had me go
First and I crashed at this turn on this sharp right turn you know on the sandy turn and anyway
Right there my bag falls off because I had my brother take all the stuff out of the bag that
The guys at the bike shop gave me and put it into my own bag because it was really big and it could hold
Like three tubes because the guys at the bike shop were like yo I'm mad you're gonna eat like three
Tubes so I put that back on the strap that holds it to the seat post was broken so it was just kind
Of dangling there and I was just like crossing my fingers and actually when I was going downhill I was
Kind of like pinching it with my legs across the back the seat just kind of like don't fall off again
Bag but I did in that lap but I was riding the lap and I felt good I felt kind of sluggish though
Because my legs were kind of like leg speed had gone away for the first time actually in
My whole bike tour because that was like the longest that I hadn't ridden um you I took like
A week off and athens a week off and albania a week and a half off in norway but other than that
I basically would ride like three days and then take two or two days off like in europe when I hit the
Capital seas and stuff but um I uh and I felt sluggish also because my stomach was like bloated
Because I I realized I had eaten like a lot but it didn't bother me I figured that was kind of cool
Because it meant that I would have extra energy for later and it would also force me to pace myself
Um so I went there and I went down I passed through and then I got back to my pit
And um I took the bag off and I was like and they had it and I was like man you guys got to
Change this to the other bag and I just left it there I just kind of put it up to chances that I
Wouldn't get a flat and I gave him my water bottle the my strategy was to bring a water bottle the first
Lap that was like three quarters full and I made sure to drink all of it um on the first lap and then
They gave me a camelback as I passed through on the first lap and um so then I did my second lap
And um I felt good but I didn't feel like I had any like leg speed and you know and all these people
Started passing me and then like at the end of the technical section this girl zooms by me
And then she gets stuck because she took the wrong line and like makes me stop
She's like oops sorry and then she like gets off and then she just zooms off just
It's like five foot tall girl and I was like holy shit um and then I did that lap and then I did the
Next lap um okay so yeah my first lap was 116 my second lap was 121 and then I did the third lap and
I'm starting to like like gradually like start to get slower and I think I didn't eat anything on my first
Lap maybe I had one gel and then my second I had like two gels and then like starting my third I
Started like making it a point to like eat gels because like what I my general rule of thumb from
Bike touring is that I would have like a really big breakfast and then ride and then like after either
40 miles or after like a two and a half hours of riding I would be ready to eat again so it's kind
Of the same kind of a situation um and so I was kind of trying to eat like four or five gels every
Single lap it's like four or five hundred calories I did a lap with this guy michael
Who's a trainer and has it all figured out um when he has this computer that has it all figured out
Based on his heart rate his age his weight his vo2 max um and his like fat percentage and like
Everything figure out how much like calories he did and he looked at his computers like oh I did
1350 calories and he was 165 pounds is like 10 pounds lighter than me and so anyways I figured I
Would be doing maybe 1400 calories or something like that 40 250 calories 1500 calories or something
Like that a lap so that's definitely not enough to I mean that that means you're gonna have those
Little packets are 100 calories each that means you're gonna have to eat like 15 packets a lap but
You can't do that your stomach can't handle that and you you would be going off of reserves anyway I think
I think I read like six months ago I have the book here I should look at it again
That your body has like 15 to 20 000 calories of reserves of like starting reserves you know
For fresh in your legs or whatever or in your whole body but I think I'm like kind of a big guy I'm 176
Pounds so maybe I have more than that so anyways um and then you would eat a little bit at the meal
So yeah you just basically eat as much as you can but um yeah and then on my third lap on the kind
Of towards the end of the last climb the first girl passes me the first girl soloist passes me
Yari kirk like I could uh yari kirkland I could I should remember to pronounce words the whole way
Before I start the next word um I could recognize her because she's on the look from the pictures on
The internet and then she was on a scott bike and she like looking forward without even looking over
She's just like good job 19 and just goes ahead so I guess that's what she does when she passes guys
But um and then uh on my fourth lap on the downhill like three quarters of the way through
So we're gonna go to second part video
So
